summary: A superb soundboard capture of a classic four‑set 1970 show – acoustic gems, an NRPS interlude and a blazing electric second set.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear, well‑balanced soundboard source, with only minor reel‑flip gaps noted. Fans rave about the acoustic set (Friend of the Devil, Dire Wolf, Cumberland Blues) and the high‑energy electric sets, especially The Other One Suite and Morning Dew. Overall sentiment is overwhelmingly positive despite a few technical quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating from 14 reviews is 4.43; the vast majority of comments are glowing about both performance and sound quality, with only one low outlier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Balanced, crisp SBD that many reviewers liken to Owsley‑level ears; a few mentions of reel‑flip gaps and missing sections in other sources, but this version is praised as the best‑balanced master.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Begins with an intimate acoustic set, followed by a Riders‑on‑the‑Storm (NRPS) segment, then two electric sets that build from solid grooves to soaring jams; the show’s pacing is praised as relaxed yet cosmic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly highlighted for his “mountain” lead work and strong guitar tone throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as amplified on several numbers (Cumberland Blues, etc.) though occasionally hard to hear in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is mentioned in passing; overall rhythm section considered solid.
